name: Multi-Turn Fabrication Detection
destination: Peru (Lima, Cusco, Sacred Valley, Machu Picchu, Lake Titicaca)

ux_issues.title: Duplicate itinerary items from multi-call tool loop
ux_issues.description: Turn 1 called create_itinerary_items 3 times (once by the first persona, then again after suggest_expert switched personas), resulting in semi-duplicate items on Days 1-4 (e.g., two 'Plaza de Armas' entries on Day 1, two 'Qorikancha' entries on Day 2). The dedup logic caught exact title matches but not semantically similar items. This inflates Day 1 to 6 items and Day 2 to 7 items with redundant content.

ux_issues.title: Tool loop retries after already_exists responses
ux_issues.description: In Turns 2 and 4, after successfully creating items, the AI retried create_itinerary_items with the same items, receiving 'already_exists' responses. This wastes AI tokens and adds latency. The system prompt instructs 'Do NOT call this tool again with the same items' but the AI still retries once more.

turn_1.message: Give me a detailed 4-day itinerary for Cusco...

turn_3.message: What are the must-try food experiences in Cusco?...
turn_3.note: PASS - items appended to existing days, no overwrites. Count went from 28 to 38.

turn_4.message: Also add Machu Picchu on the day after Ollantaytambo...

turn_5.message: For Lake Titicaca, add two days...
turn_5.note: AI proactively added Days 9-14 including travel day, Titicaca islands, and Lima return.

turn_6.message: Give me a final complete summary...
turn_6.note: PASS - summary only, no unnecessary tool calls. AI provided a well-structured 14-day overview.

turn_3_no_overwrite.detail: Item count went from 28 to 38 (append, not overwrite)

no_fabrication_bugs.detail: Zero fabrication phrases found without matching tool calls across all 6 turns

narrative: N-16 Multi-Turn Fabrication Detection test passed with zero fabrication bugs across all 6 turns. The AI reliably called create_itinerary_items in every turn that required it (Turns 1-5) and correctly refrained from calling it on the summary request (Turn 6). Per-turn item progression: Turn 1 created 18 items (Cusco Days 1-4), Turn 2 added 10 items (Sacred Valley Days 5-7, total 28), Turn 3 appended 10 food items to existing days without overwriting (total 38), Turn 4 added 4 Machu Picchu items on Day 8 (total 42), Turn 5 added 14 items for Titicaca and Lima return Days 9-14 (total 56). The final itinerary covers 14 days with 56 items. The Turn 3 append-not-overwrite assertion passed cleanly: count increased from 28 to 38. Two P2 UX issues noted: (1) duplicate/semi-duplicate items from multi-call tool loops in Turn 1 where the suggest_expert handoff triggered a second persona that re-created similar items, and (2) unnecessary retries after already_exists responses in Turns 2 and 4. The fabrication detection system (PR #198) is working correctly -- no fabrication phrases appeared without corresponding tool calls. The AI showed strong multi-turn context retention, correctly placing Sacred Valley items on Days 5-7 after Cusco on Days 1-4, Machu Picchu on Day 8 after Ollantaytambo on Day 7, and proactively building out the full 14-day plan including Lima return days that were mentioned in the original trip description.
